2603.16848.title: Mediocrity is the key for LLM as a Judge Anchor Selection
2603.16848.paper_summary: The ``LLM-as-a-judge'' paradigm has become a standard method for evaluating open-ended generation. To address the quadratic scalability costs of pairwise comparisons, popular benchmarks like Arena-Hard and AlpacaEval compare all models against a single anchor. However, despite its widespread use, the impact of anchor selection on the reliability of the results remains largely unexplored. In this work, we systematically investigate the effect of anchor selection by evaluating 22 different anchors on the Arena-Hard-v2.0 dataset. We find that the choice of anchor is critical: a poor anchor can dramatically reduce correlation with human rankings. We identify that common anchor choices (best-performing and worst-performing models) make poor anchors. Because these extreme anchors are consistently better or worse than all other models, they are seldom indicative of the relative ranking of the models. We further quantify the effect size of anchor selection, showing it is comparable to the selection of a judge model. We conclude with actionable recommendations. First, we conduct a power analysis, and compute sufficient benchmark sizes for anchor-based evaluation, finding that standard benchmark sizes are insufficient for pairwise evaluation and fail to distinguish between competitive models reliably. Second, we provide guidelines for selecting informative anchors to ensure reliable and efficient evaluation practices.
2603.16848.paper_summary_zh:
```
``LLM-as-a-judge'' 範式已成為評估開放式生成的標準方法。為了應對成對比較的二次擴展成本，像 Arena-Hard 和 AlpacaEval 這樣的流行基準將所有模型與單一錨點進行比較。然而，儘管其被廣泛使用，錨點選擇對結果可靠性的影響仍然在很大程度上未被探索。在本研究中，我們系統地調查了錨點選擇的影響，評估了 Arena-Hard-v2.0 數據集上的 22 個不同錨點。我們發現錨點的選擇至關重要：不良的錨點會顯著降低與人類排名的相關性。我們識別出常見的錨點選擇（表現最佳和表現最差的模型）實際上是劣質錨點。因為這些極端錨點始終比所有其他模型表現更好或更差，所以它們很少能反映模型的相對排名。我們進一步量化了錨點選擇的效應大小，顯示其與選擇評判模型相當。我們最後提出可行的建議。首先，我們進行了功效分析，並計算了基於錨點評估的足夠基準大小，發現標準基準大小對於成對評估來說不足，並無法可靠地區分競爭模型。其次，我們提供了選擇信息性錨點的指導方針，以確保可靠和高效的評估實踐。
```
2603.16848.author: Shachar Don-Yehiya et.al.
2603.16848.authors: Shachar Don-Yehiya, Asaf Yehudai, Leshem Choshen, Omri Abend
